string-array.name: recipe_borsch_ingredients
string-array.item: Sour cream and dill to serve

string-array.name: recipe_borsch_steps
string-array.item: Place beef in a pot with 2.5 L water. Bring to boil, skim foam. Cook 60 min.
Roast beets in oven at 200°C for 40 min, then peel and grate.
Sauté onion and carrot in oil for 5 min. Add tomato paste and vinegar.
Remove beef, shred it. Add potatoes to broth and cook 15 min.
Add cabbage and cook 10 more minutes.
Add sautéed vegetables and grated beets. Return beef. Season to taste.
Simmer 10 min. Serve hot with sour cream and fresh dill.

string-array.name: recipe_granola_ingredients
string-array.item: Greek yogurt and fresh berries for serving

string-array.name: recipe_granola_steps
string-array.item: Mix oats, nuts, cinnamon in a bowl.
Whisk together honey, olive oil and vanilla. Pour over oat mixture.
Spread onto a baking sheet and bake for 20-25 minutes, stirring halfway.
Let cool completely — it will crisp up as it cools.
Serve over yogurt topped with fresh berries.

string-array.name: recipe_omelette_steps
string-array.item: Crack eggs into a bowl, add salt, pepper and milk. Whisk well.
Melt butter in a pan over medium-low heat.
Pour in egg mixture and stir gently with a spatula.
When edges start to set, stop stirring and let the center cook.
Sprinkle herbs, fold the omelette in half and slide onto a plate.

string-array.name: recipe_pancakes_steps
string-array.item: Beat eggs with sugar and salt in a large bowl.
Gradually add milk while whisking continuously.
Sift flour into the mixture and stir until smooth. Add vegetable oil.
Let the batter rest for 15 minutes.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and melt a little butter.
Pour a thin layer of batter and tilt the pan to spread evenly.
Cook each side for 1-2 minutes until golden. Serve with jam, honey or sour cream.

string-array.name: recipe_pasta_steps
string-array.item: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup pasta water.
Fry guanciale in a dry pan over medium heat until crispy.
Beat egg yolks with whole egg, add Pecorino and plenty of black pepper.
Drain pasta, add to pan with guanciale (heat off).
Pour egg mixture over pasta, toss quickly. Add pasta water little by little to create creamy sauce.
Serve immediately with extra Pecorino and pepper.

string-array.name: recipe_pelmeni_steps
string-array.item: Mix flour, egg, water and salt into a firm dough. Knead 10 min. Rest 30 min.
Combine ground meats, grated onion, salt and pepper for filling.
Roll dough thin and cut circles with a glass (6-7 cm).
Place a teaspoon of filling in centre of each circle. Fold and seal edges well.
Bring a large pot of salted water to boil. Cook pelmeni in batches.
They are ready 3-4 minutes after they float to the surface.
Serve with sour cream, butter or mustard.

string-array.name: recipe_pizza_steps
string-array.item: Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water, let stand 10 min until foamy.
Mix flour and salt, add yeast mixture and olive oil. Knead 10 min until smooth.
Let dough rise in a warm place for 1 hour, covered.
Simmer crushed tomatoes with garlic, salt and basil for 15 min. Let cool.
Preheat oven to 250°C (480°F). Use highest setting with baking stone or sheet.
Stretch dough into a 30 cm circle. Spread sauce, add torn mozzarella.
Bake 10-12 minutes until crust is charred at edges. Top with fresh basil.

string-array.name: recipe_salmon_ingredients
string-array.item: 2 salmon fillets (180 g each), skin on

string-array.name: recipe_salmon_steps
string-array.item: Pat salmon dry with paper towels. Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a pan over high heat until shimmering.
Place salmon skin-side down. Press gently and cook 4-5 minutes without moving.
Add butter, garlic and herbs. Tilt pan and baste salmon with melted butter.
Squeeze lemon over. Serve with roasted vegetables or salad.

string-array.name: recipe_schnitzel_steps
string-array.item: Pound veal cutlets between plastic wrap to 3-4 mm thickness.
Coat in flour, dip in beaten eggs, then coat in breadcrumbs. Press gently.
Heat oil to 170°C in a wide pan. Oil should be 1 cm deep.
Fry schnitzel for 2-3 minutes per side, shaking pan gently for even browning.
Drain on paper towel. Serve immediately with lemon wedges and potato salad.

resources.string: Discover recipes from around the world
Traditional Eastern European beet soup, rich, hearty and deeply flavourful.
Crunchy homemade granola with oats, nuts and honey, served with yogurt.
A classic soft French omelette with herbs — ready in under 15 minutes.
Thin and delicate Russian crepes, perfect for breakfast with honey or jam.
Authentic Italian pasta with creamy egg sauce, guanciale and Pecorino cheese.
Traditional Russian dumplings filled with mixed meat — the ultimate comfort food.
Neapolitan pizza with homemade dough, San Marzano tomato sauce and fresh mozzarella.
Restaurant-style salmon with crispy skin, lemon butter and fresh herbs.
Classic Austrian veal schnitzel — crispy, golden and irresistibly tender.
